Shape Support

Complete documentation for shapes in Aspose.Cells FOSS for .NET

← Back to Index

Shape Overview

The Shape class represents a drawing object (auto shape) anchored to a worksheet with customizable geometry and properties.

Shape Types

Aspose.Cells FOSS supports 30+ different shape types:

Basic Shapes
  • Rectangle: Standard rectangle
  • Rounded Rectangle: Rectangle with rounded corners
  • Ellipse: Oval/circle
  • Triangle: Triangle
  • Diamond: Diamond shape
  • Pentagon: Five-sided polygon
  • Hexagon: Six-sided polygon
  • Octagon: Eight-sided polygon
Arrows
  • Right Arrow: Arrow pointing right
  • Left Arrow: Arrow pointing left
  • Up Arrow: Arrow pointing up
  • Down Arrow: Arrow pointing down
  • Left-Right Arrow: Double-headed arrow (left and right)
  • Up-Down Arrow: Double-headed arrow (up and down)
Connectors
  • Straight Connector: Straight line
  • Bent Connector: Elbow connector
  • Curved Connector: Curved line
Symbols
  • Plus: Plus sign
  • Cube: 3D cube
  • Cylinder: Cylinder
  • Heart: Heart shape
  • Lightning: Lightning bolt
  • Sun: Sun
  • Moon: Crescent moon
  • Cloud: Cloud
Stars
  • Star 4 to 32 Points: Various star shapes
Advanced
  • Text Box: For adding text
  • Math Plus: Plus mathematical symbol

Usage Examples

Creating Various Shapes

using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;

var workbook = new Workbook();
var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];

// Add different shapes
worksheet.Shapes.Add(5, 5, 7, 7, AutoShapeType.Rectangle);
worksheet.Shapes.Add(15, 5, 12, 7, AutoShapeType.Ellipse);
worksheet.Shapes.Add(25, 5, 17, 7, AutoShapeType.Triangle);
worksheet.Shapes.Add(35, 5, 22, 7, AutoShapeType.RightArrow);
worksheet.Shapes.Add(5, 5, 27, 7, AutoShapeType.TextBox);
worksheet.Shapes.Add(15, 5, 32, 7, AutoShapeType.Star5Point);

Console.WriteLine($"Added {worksheet.Shapes.Count} shapes");

Accessing Shape Properties

// Access a shape
var shape = worksheet.Shapes[0];

Console.WriteLine($"Shape: {shape.Name}");
Console.WriteLine($"Type: {shape.AutoShapeType}");
Console.WriteLine($"Position: ({shape.UpperLeftRow}, {shape.UpperLeftColumn}) - ({shape.LowerRightRow}, {shape.LowerRightColumn})");

// Modify shape properties
shape.Name = "Updated Shape";
shape.UpperLeftRow = 10;
shape.UpperLeftColumn = 10;   // Move to row 10, column 10

Shape Properties

The Shape class provides the following properties:

  • Name - Display name of the shape
  • AutoShapeType - Geometry type (Rectangle, Ellipse, Triangle, etc.)
  • GeometryType - Raw DrawingML prst string
  • UpperLeftRow - Zero-based row of upper-left anchor
  • UpperLeftColumn - Zero-based column of upper-left anchor
  • LowerRightRow - Zero-based row of lower-right anchor
  • LowerRightColumn - Zero-based column of lower-right anchor
  • Position - Anchor position (row and column)

ShapeCollection

The ShapeCollection class manages all shapes in a worksheet:

  • Count - Number of shapes
  • this[int] - Access shape by index
  • Add() - Add new shape with type and coordinates
  • RemoveAt() - Remove shape at index
  • Parameters: position coordinates and shape type

AutoShapeType Enumeration

Complete enumeration of 30+ supported shape types:

  • Basic: Rectangle, Ellipse, Triangle, Diamond, Pentagon, Hexagon, Octagon
  • Stars: Star4, Star5, Star6, Star7, Star8, Star10, Star12, Star16, Star24, Star32
  • Arrows: Right, Left, Up, Down, Left-Right, Up-Down
  • Connectors: Straight, Bent, Curved
  • Symbols: Plus, Cube, Cylinder, Heart, Lightning, Sun, Moon, Cloud
  • Text Box: TextBox (distinct type)
  • Math: Math Plus (distinct type)

Important Notes

  • Positioning: Shapes are positioned using zero-based row and column coordinates
  • Anchor Points: Upper-left and lower-right coordinates define the shape's position
  • Shape Size: The size is determined by the distance between anchor points
  • Geometry Types: Setting AutoShapeType automatically updates the GeometryType property
